process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan"). TAL spreadsheet files and Design Brief files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Do not process any document tagged as 'Design Brief'. For each Site Plan document, run the two-phase extraction defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info. Phase 1 extracts all numbered notes and callout annotations (ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION/NOTICE) from every page of the drawing in full. Phase 2 analyses those extracted notes to produce four structured checks: (1) Substation Data — extract the seven Substation Notes fields (Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear with 1.5MVA Siemens RLR override rule, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing); (2) Assets Field Check Compliance — verify note 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' is present and answered YES; (3) Easement Restriction — check for the swimming pool restriction-on-use-of-land easement note; (4) Easement Substation — check for the padmount substation easement note with dimensions and lot number.

expected_output.description: Step 3: Extract Site Plan Information — Five structured extraction outputs per document: complete drawing notes, substation structured data, field check compliance result, and two easement check results.

fields.sub-step-extract-all-notes: Complete list of all numbered notes and standalone callouts from every page, in sequence order
fields.sub-step-substation-data: Formatted string with all seven Substation Notes fields, with 1.5MVA override notation where applicable
fields.sub-step-field-check: 'YES', 'NO', or 'NOT FOUND' result for the assets field-check compliance note
fields.sub-step-easement-restriction: Extracted restriction-on-use-of-land note text with lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'
fields.sub-step-easement-substation: Extracted padmount substation easement note text with dimensions and lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract All Drawing Notes and Annotations
sub_steps.details: Extract every textual note and annotation from every page of the drawing in full. This is a comprehensive raw extraction pass — do not filter or summarise. Capture: all numbered notes (1., 2., 3., ...) from the NOTES section with their complete text; all standalone ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, and NOTICE: callout blocks with their complete text; any inline hand-written or red-line markup annotations. Notes that span multiple drawing regions must be assembled into a single complete entry. This output is the source material for all Phase 2 sub-steps.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate and Extract Numbered Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan every page of the drawing for a dedicated NOTES section or numbered annotation block. Extract ALL individual notes listed, in sequence order (1., 2., 3., ...). Each note must be captured in full — do not truncate. If a note is split across adjacent image regions (common in large-format drawings), assemble the complete text. Include every note number in the final output — missing numbers indicate an incomplete extraction.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Standalone Callout Annotations
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: In addition to the numbered notes block, scan every page for standalone callout boxes or annotations with labels ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, or NOTICE:. These may appear anywhere on the drawing (not just in the NOTES block) and often contain critical safety, service, or access requirements. Extract each callout's label and its complete text. If the callout appears physically separate from the NOTES block, include it as an additional entry appended after the numbered notes (e.g. 'CAUTION: HIGH PRESSURE GAS MAINS IN VICINITY...').

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Red-line and Hand-written Markups
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Look for hand-written or red-line markup annotations that modify or supplement the printed notes — for example, specific cable types, removal or addition instructions, or project-specific notes. Capture these verbatim and identify them as markups (e.g. '[MARKUP] Remove span between...').

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, locate the Substation Notes annotation block and extract the seven structured data fields. Apply the conditional HV Switchgear rule: if Transformer Size is 1.5MVA, HV Switchgear must be recorded as 'Siemens RLR' regardless of what is written in the drawing, and the override must be explicitly noted in the output. Return all seven fields as a formatted multi-line string.
sub_steps.output_format:
Multi-line formatted string:
Substation Asset Number: [value or NOT FOUND]
Transformer Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
HV Switchgear: [value — if 1.5MVA rule applied, append: OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required]
Voltage Level: [value or NOT FOUND]
LV Switchgear: [value or NOT FOUND]
Cubicle Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
Earthing: [value or NOT FOUND]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Substation Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search the extracted notes and the drawing for annotations labelled 'Substation Notes', 'Sub Notes', or a similar heading. These are typically found in a table or callout box near the substation symbol or in the drawing title block area. Extract the heading and all fields within it.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract the Seven Data Fields
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: From the Substation Notes block, extract each of the following fields as labelled: Substation Asset Number (unique ID assigned by the network operator, e.g. S1234 or a 6-digit number), Transformer Size (rated capacity with units, e.g. 1.5MVA, 500kVA, 200kVA), HV Switchgear (make and model, e.g. Siemens RLR, ABB SafeRing, or as written), Voltage Level (in kV, e.g. 11kV, 22kV, 33kV), LV Switchgear (make, model, and rating), Cubicle Size (dimensions or type code), and Earthing (type and compliance standard).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Apply 1.5MVA Conditional HV Switchgear Rule
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Check the extracted Transformer Size. If it equals 1.5MVA (or equivalently 1500kVA), then regardless of what the drawing shows for HV Switchgear, record HV Switchgear as 'Siemens RLR' and append the note 'OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required.' to that field. If the transformer size is not 1.5MVA, record HV Switchgear exactly as written in the Substation Notes.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, verify that the drawing includes the mandatory assets field-check compliance note. The exact note required is: 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' answered with 'YES'. Return one of three values: 'YES' (note present, answered YES), 'NO' (note present, answered NO — flag as non-compliant), or 'NOT FOUND' (note is absent from the drawing — flag for follow-up).
sub_steps.required_note_text: HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Field Check Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for the text 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' or a close paraphrase. This note is typically numbered within the NOTES section and followed by 'YES' or 'NO'. Note: in the drawing the word 'DRAWING' may appear as 'DESIGN' — treat both as equivalent.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Read and Return the Answer
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Once the note is located, read the answer that follows it ('YES' or 'NO'). Return 'YES' if the answer is YES. Return 'NO' if the answer is NO (and add a flag: 'NON-COMPLIANT — field check not confirmed'). If the note cannot be found anywhere in the drawing, return 'NOT FOUND' (and add a flag: 'MISSING — this compliance note is mandatory').

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Easement — Restriction on Use of Land (Swimming Pool Setback)
sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required restriction-on-use-of-land note relating to swimming pools within the substation easement setback. This note is required for any project that creates a substation easement. Extract the full note text including the lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent, determine whether this is a padmount substation project — if so, flag as MISSING; if not, flag as NOT REQUIRED.
sub_steps.required_note_template: A RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND, IN RELATION TO SWIMMING POOLS, MEASURED 5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a substation easement project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (substation easement project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Restriction Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND', 'SWIMMING POOL', '5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT', or 'IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a separate annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Note Text and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Identify and record the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' — this is a critical compliance detail. Return the complete extracted text as the field value.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required padmount substation easement creation note. This note is required for all projects involving a padmount substation. Extract the full note text including the easement dimensions and lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent and the project involves a padmount substation, flag as MISSING.
sub_steps.required_note_template: AN EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM [x]m x [x]m IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with easement dimensions and lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a padmount substation project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (padmount substation project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Padmount Easement Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION', 'PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM', or 'CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' alongside a dimension or measurement. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a standalone annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Easement Dimensions and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Specifically identify: (a) the easement dimensions stated after 'MINIMUM' (e.g. '3m x 4m', '4.5m x 6m') and (b) the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. Both details are critical — record them clearly in the output.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

(i) NOTICE OF INTENT HAS NOT BEEN RECEIVED WITHIN SIX (6) MONTHS OF THIS CERTIFICATION, OR
(ii) CONSTRUCTION HAS BEEN INTERRUPTED FOR MORE THAN SIX (6) MONTHS.

-MOBILE GENERATORS AND SUBSTATIONS
